tasks.name: Set up a $25+ monthly donation to the Youth Scholarship Fund starting next month
tasks.description: Create a recurring monthly donation to the 'Youth Scholarship Fund' by selecting the lowest suggested monthly amount that is at least $25, setting the start date to the first day of next month, choosing to cover processing fees, and submitting the on-site donation form up to the final review page (no external payment service).
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Donate" link in the main header navigation.
On the donation page, click the "Fund" dropdown and select "Youth Scholarship Fund" from the list of available funds.
Find the donation frequency options and select the "Monthly" or "Recurring monthly" option.
In the suggested monthly amounts list (for example, $10, $25, $50, $100), identify the smallest amount that is at least $25 and click that amount option.
If an additional amount field is shown, leave it unchanged and scroll to the "Start Date" or "Begin On" dropdown.
Open the "Start Date" dropdown and select the first day of the next calendar month (for example, "July 1" if the current month is June).
Locate the option labeled "Cover processing fees" or similar and select the checkbox to indicate that you will cover the fees.

Fill in the required donor information fields on this page (such as first name, last name, and email address) using any placeholder values, and leave any optional organization or company fields blank.
Click the "Review Donation" or equivalent button to proceed to the on-site donation review page without continuing to any external payment provider.

tasks.name: Register a 10-year-old for the earliest weekday evening audition next month at the Main Campus
tasks.description: Find the youth choir audition schedule, filter for auditions at the 'Main Campus' location in the next calendar month, and register a 10-year-old child for the earliest available weekday evening time slot starting at or after 5:00 PM.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation, hover over or click the "Join" or "Auditions" menu and select the "Youth Choir Auditions" option.
On the auditions page, locate the age or grade filter and select the option appropriate for a 10-year-old (for example, "Ages 9–11" or "Intermediate Choir").
Find the location filter dropdown or buttons and select "Main Campus" as the audition location.
Use the date picker or month navigation control to move to the next calendar month from today.

Apply any available time-of-day or "Evening" filter; if there is no dedicated filter, visually scan the listed time slots and focus only on weekday options (Monday through Friday) that start at or after 5:00 PM.

Among the eligible weekday evening time slots in that next month, identify the earliest date and within that date the earliest listed qualifying time (for example, choose June 3 at 5:30 PM over June 7 at 6:00 PM).
Click on that selected time slot to open the audition registration form.
In the registration form, fill in the child’s name, select age as "10", and enter a placeholder parent or guardian contact phone and email in the corresponding fields.
Click the "Submit Audition Registration" or equivalent button to complete the on-site registration for that specific time slot.

tasks.name: Submit a need-based tuition scholarship application for the 2025–2026 season
tasks.description: Complete and submit the online scholarship form for a need-based tuition scholarship for the 2025–2026 choir season, choosing the correct scholarship type, selecting a household income range below $60,000, indicating that the singer is a current member, and providing short text responses where required.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation, click on the "Scholarships" or "Financial Aid" menu item.
On the scholarships overview page, click the link or button labeled "Apply Now" or "Tuition Scholarship Application" for the 2025–2026 season.
On the application form’s first section, select the scholarship type option labeled "Need-based tuition scholarship" or the closest equivalent from the available scholarship types.
In the season or year dropdown, choose "2025–2026" as the season you are applying for.
Find the question asking about the singer’s current status and select the option indicating that the singer is a current choir member (for example, "Yes, currently enrolled").
Locate the household income range field and select a range whose upper limit is less than $60,000 (for example, "$40,000–$59,999"), ignoring any higher-income options.
Fill in the required personal information fields such as singer’s name, date of birth, and guardian contact details using placeholder values, leaving any optional secondary contact fields blank.
In at least one short-answer or explanation field (for example, "Describe your financial need"), type a brief placeholder explanation of 2–3 sentences, ensuring the text box is not left empty.
Scroll to the end of the form and click the "Submit Scholarship Application" or similar button to send the completed application through the site.

tasks.name: Purchase two mid-priced tickets for the December Holiday Concert in the same row
tasks.description: Find the December Holiday Concert event, select the mid-priced ticket tier (neither the cheapest nor the most expensive) under $50 per ticket, choose two adjacent seats in the same row, and proceed through the on-site checkout up to the final order summary page (no external payment service).
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Concerts" or "Events" menu item in the main navigation to go to the events listing.
Use any available date filter or month selector to display events in December of the current year.
From the December events, locate the one titled "Holiday Concert" or closest matching title and click its event card to open the event details page.
On the event details page, click the "Buy Tickets" or "Select Seats" button.
On the ticket selection screen, review the available price tiers and identify the mid-priced tier that is under $50 per ticket (for example, if tiers are $20, $35, and $60, choose the $35 tier).
Select that mid-priced tier and, if a seating chart is shown, choose two adjacent seats in the same row within that price tier by clicking on two neighboring seat icons.
Set the number of tickets to "2" in any quantity dropdown or ticket count field, making sure it corresponds to the two selected seats.
Click the "Add to Cart" or "Continue" button to move to the on-site checkout or order summary page.

On the checkout form, fill in the required buyer information fields such as name and email with placeholder data, then click the "Review Order" or equivalent button to reach the final order summary page without leaving the site.

tasks.name: Sign up for a Saturday volunteer usher shift of at least 3 hours this month
tasks.description: Browse volunteer opportunities, filter to in-person event support roles, and register for a Saturday shift occurring later this month that lasts at least 3 hours and is labeled as an usher or front-of-house role.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation, click the "Get Involved" or "Volunteer" menu item.
On the volunteer opportunities page, use the category or role type filter to select "Event Support" or a similar category that includes usher roles.
Use the date filter or month selector to show opportunities in the current month and scroll until you see listings for Saturdays.
Among the Saturday listings, identify a role whose title or description mentions "Usher" or "Front-of-House" and whose listed duration is at least 3 hours (for example, 4:00 PM–7:30 PM).
Click on that specific volunteer opportunity to open the detailed description page.
On the details page, click the "Sign Up" or "Volunteer for This Shift" button.
Fill out the volunteer sign-up form with placeholder values for name, email, and phone number, leaving any optional notes field blank.
If a checkbox asking to receive volunteer updates or newsletters is present, select it to opt in.
Click the "Submit" or "Confirm Volunteer Registration" button to complete the on-site sign-up for that shift.

tasks.name: Choose a choir ensemble for a 13-year-old with rehearsals ending by 7:30 PM and tuition under $600, then start registration
tasks.description: Compare available youth ensembles, identify one that accepts 13-year-olds, has regular rehearsals ending no later than 7:30 PM on weekdays, and lists annual tuition under $600, and then begin the registration process for that specific ensemble.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the "Choirs" or "Programs" menu item and then select the "Youth Ensembles" or equivalent option.
On the ensembles overview page, locate and use any filter or dropdown for age or grade level to narrow results to ensembles that include age 13 (for example, select "Middle School" or "Ages 12–14").
Open at least two ensemble detail pages in turn by clicking their names, and for each one, read the rehearsal schedule information to identify whether rehearsals end by 7:30 PM on weekdays.
For each ensemble you view, also locate the tuition or annual fee section and note whether the amount listed is less than $600 for the season.

From the ensembles that both accept 13-year-olds and meet the constraints of ending rehearsals by 7:30 PM and having tuition under $600, choose one that best fits these requirements and return to that ensemble’s detail page.
On the chosen ensemble’s detail page, click the "Register" or "Enroll" button for the upcoming season.
On the registration form, select the appropriate season (for example, "2025–2026") from any season dropdown.
Fill in the singer’s basic information fields such as name, age as "13", and school name using placeholder values.
Click the "Continue" or "Save and Continue" button to advance to the next step of the on-site registration process without leaving the website.

tasks.name: Subscribe to the email newsletter with specific interests and a monthly frequency
tasks.description: Use the website’s on-site subscription form to sign up for the email newsletter using a placeholder email address, select interests in both 'Upcoming Concerts' and 'Scholarships & Financial Aid', and choose a 'Monthly' or equivalent digest frequency.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Scroll to the footer or locate the "Newsletter" or "Stay Informed" link and click it to open the email subscription page or form.
In the email address field, type a placeholder email such as "sample.user@example.com".
In the list of interest checkboxes or topic selections, select the options labeled "Upcoming Concerts" and "Scholarships & Financial Aid" or the closest matching labels.
If other interest options are selected by default, deselect them so that only "Upcoming Concerts" and "Scholarships & Financial Aid" remain selected.
Find the frequency dropdown or radio buttons and choose the "Monthly" or "Monthly Digest" option, avoiding daily or weekly options.
Enter placeholder values in any required name fields, such as first and last name, leaving optional organization fields blank.
If a checkbox is present to agree to terms or privacy policy, select it to indicate agreement.
Click the "Subscribe", "Sign Up", or equivalent button to submit the newsletter preferences through the on-site form.

tasks.name: Reserve a spot in a free adult community singing workshop downtown next month
tasks.description: Locate community workshops, filter for free adult workshops scheduled for next month at the Downtown location, and reserve one seat in a qualifying workshop using the on-site registration system.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation, click on the "Education" or "Community Programs" menu and select the "Workshops" or "Community Singing" option.
On the workshops listing page, use the age group filter to select "Adult" or "18+" workshops.
Use the fee or price filter to show only workshops labeled as "Free" or with a price of $0.
Use the date picker or month selector to move to the next calendar month and apply it so only workshops in that month are displayed.
Apply the location filter and select the "Downtown" location or venue from the list of available locations.
From the filtered workshop list, choose any one workshop that matches all criteria (adult, free, next month, Downtown) by clicking its title or "View Details" link.
On the workshop detail page, click the "Register" or "Reserve Spot" button.
On the registration form, set the number of participants or seats to "1" if a quantity field is available, and fill in the required contact fields such as name and email with placeholder information.
Click the "Complete Registration" or "Confirm Reservation" button to finalize the on-site reservation for that workshop.

entities.name: DonationFund
entities.description: Designated funds donors can give to, such as the Youth Scholarship Fund.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Public fund name, e.g., 'Youth Scholarship Fund'

fields.name: is_default
fields.description: Whether this is the default selected fund

entities.name: SuggestedDonationAmount
entities.description: Suggested donation amounts per fund and frequency (e.g., $10, $25, $50 for monthly gifts).

entities.name: Donation
entities.description: Configured on-site donation before any external payment; supports one-time and recurring gifts.

fields.name: start_date
fields.description: For recurring donations: first charge date

entities.name: Location
entities.description: Physical locations such as Main Campus and Downtown used for auditions, events, rehearsals, and workshops.

entities.name: AuditionSlot
entities.description: Individual youth choir audition time slots with age, location, and timing metadata.

fields.name: program_name
fields.description: Name or level, e.g., 'Youth Choir Audition – Ages 9–11'

fields.name: seasonId
fields.description: Optional Season.id if tied to a season

entities.name: AuditionRegistration
entities.description: On-site registration submitted for a specific audition slot.

entities.name: ScholarshipType
entities.description: Types of scholarships or tuition aid available.

entities.name: Season
entities.description: Choir seasons such as 2025–2026, shared across scholarships, ensembles, and events.

entities.name: IncomeRangeOption
entities.description: Household income ranges shown as fixed options in scholarship applications.

entities.name: ScholarshipApplication
entities.description: Need-based tuition scholarship or other scholarship application submissions.

entities.name: Event
entities.description: Concerts and other public events, such as the December Holiday Concert.

entities.name: TicketPriceTier
entities.description: Price levels for event tickets (e.g., $20, $35, $60) used to identify mid-priced tiers.

entities.name: Seat
entities.description: Individual seats for an event, including row/seat numbers and price tier assignment.

entities.name: TicketCart
entities.description: Single-user ticket cart used during seat selection and checkout.

fields.name: items
fields.description: Optional denormalized list of CartItem summaries

entities.name: CartItem
entities.description: Line item in the ticket cart representing selected seats for an event and price tier.

entities.name: TicketOrder
entities.description: On-site ticket order created at checkout and shown on the order review page (no external payment).

entities.name: TicketOrderItem
entities.description: Individual tickets within a ticket order, including seat details.

entities.name: VolunteerOpportunity
entities.description: Volunteer roles and specific shifts, including usher/front-of-house event support.

entities.name: VolunteerRegistration
entities.description: Volunteer sign-up for a specific opportunity/shift.

entities.name: Ensemble
entities.description: Youth choir ensembles including age eligibility, rehearsal schedule, and tuition.

entities.name: EnsembleRegistration
entities.description: Start of an ensemble registration for a singer for a specific season.

entities.name: NewsletterInterestOption
entities.description: Predefined newsletter interest topics users can select, such as Upcoming Concerts or Scholarships & Financial Aid.

entities.name: NewsletterFrequencyOption
entities.description: Available email sending frequencies for the newsletter.

entities.name: NewsletterSubscription
entities.description: On-site newsletter subscription capturing email, interests, and frequency.

entities.name: Workshop
entities.description: Community workshops and community singing sessions, including free adult workshops.

entities.name: WorkshopRegistration
entities.description: Reservations for community workshops including free adult sessions.
